puzzle0.title: ARCHIVE DECRYPTION
puzzle0.description: Encrypted archive device detected. Hale must stay near the terminal while you recover the printed access key.

event4.text: I thought that would make the room feel safer.

event8.text: The relay room is colder now. Or I am noticing it more. Hard to tell the difference when your hands will not stop shaking.

event10.text: There is a service corridor outside. Archive Sector C should be below this deck, past the old junction.

event12.text: I remember that junction. Coffee cart. Bad lighting. Rivera laughing at his own jokes.

event15.text: If I am wrong about the route, we will know quickly.

choice0.text: WHAT ARE YOU NOT TELLING ME?

event3.text: I keep circling one memory and finding the floor missing in the middle.

event5.text: A terminal. Red emergency light on the screen. My hand over a confirm key.

event7.text: Incident Alpha was on that screen. I remember the deletion prompt.

event10.text: I do not remember why I pressed it.

event12.text: That is the part that scares me. Not the deletion. The clean empty space where the reason should be.

event3.text: The corridor outside is darker than before. One emergency strip is blinking near the floor.

event5.text: Dust on the deck. Footprints through it. Mine, I think.

event8.text: I am choosing to believe they are mine until the station ruins that for me.

event10.text: I need both hands for the next stretch. I am going quiet.

event3.text: I do not like this choice. I understand it, but I do not like it.

event5.text: Fast route means fewer minutes for the archive cache to decay. It also means if anything is above me, it has a straight line down.

event7.text: Estimated crossing: two minutes if the ceiling remembers its job.

event3.text: I am opening the shaft. It smells like hot cable insulation and old dust.

event5.text: This is the route maintenance crews used when the lifts were down. Noah hated it. Said it was designed by someone who had never met shoulders.

event7.text: He was right about that. I am currently negotiating with a ladder, a tool belt, and my own ribs.

event9.text: Descent should take five or six minutes. I am going quiet. Both hands on the ladder. Radio clipped to my suit.

event1.text: Archive access door is in front of me.

event3.text: The paint is blistered around the lock. Not melted from outside. Pushed outward from heat inside the frame.

event5.text: There is a small inspection window in the door. Frosted over from the inside. I wiped a corner clear with my glove.

event8.text: I wish I had not done that.

event10.text: There are rows of archive columns inside. Most are dark. One is blinking blue at the far end.

event12.text: Panel is awake. One amber light. One red. Amber means waiting. Red means it already decided I am the problem.

event14.text: I can scan the door for quarantine triggers, or force it before the security routine fully wakes up.

event16.text: Scan is safer. Force is faster. Neither is clean.

event3.text: Scanner against the frame. The metal is warm through the glove. That should not happen on a sealed archive door.
I am bracing the panel with my left hand and cutting power to the latch with my right.

event8.text: Quarantine latch jammed halfway. I got the door open, but the system marked the active cache as unstable.

event10.text: Translation: some evidence may degrade while we are inside. That is the price of speed.

event12.text: I am not blaming you. I am noting the invoice.

event1.text: I am inside archive sector C.

event3.text: Rows of storage columns. Most are dark. Some are blinking under frost like they are trying to breathe.

event5.text: The floor crunches under my boots. Ice crystals. Paper scraps. A cracked ID badge with the photo burned out.

event7.text: Someone taped manual labels to a few cabinets. That is not protocol. That is what people do when they stop trusting the system.

event9.text: I see initials: E.H., N.V., M.C.

event14.text: N.V. might be Noah Voss. Maintenance systems. Bad singing voice. Worse coffee opinions.

event16.text: M.C. I do not know why that makes me uneasy. I know the initials. I cannot place the face.

event19.text: There. Portable storage device. Still powered.

event20.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
event20.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decrypt attempts may trigger quarantine response.

Status: Locked
event20.caption: A portable archive device recovered inside Sector C.

event22.text: It wants a key. The label says ALPHA. Not subtle, but neither is a locked box in a room full of dead records.

event4.text: Do not guess. The device is not clever. It is literal, and literal machines are the worst kind of stubborn.

event6.text: I am keeping the quarantine loop busy, but I can feel the terminal heating through my glove.

event8.text: We get one clean retry before the system starts eating its own cache.

choice1.text: ASK HALE TO READ THE DEVICE AGAIN.

event3.text: Archive Key Hint: ALPHA.

event5.text: Not 'Incident Alpha.' Not a sentence. Just the key word: ALPHA.

event7.text: This is probably not a puzzle. It is probably a warning wearing a password.

choice1.text: LET HALE STEADY THE TERMINAL FIRST.

event3.text: My hands were starting to shake. I was pretending they were not, but the terminal has better eyesight than my pride.

event6.text: I am taking ten seconds. Breathing in. Breathing out. Listening to the archive hum like it has a secret.

event0.title: INCIDENT ALPHA PARTIAL TRANSCRIPT
event0.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:15 — Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:19 — Signal interruption.
21:20 — Casualty report created.
21:22 — Archive deletion command issued.

NOTE: Transcript incomplete. Visual, personnel, and identity data missing.
event0.caption: Partial transcript recovered from the encrypted archive device.
event0.message: [INCIDENT ALPHA PARTIAL TRANSCRIPT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: And before you ask: yes, I know exactly how bad that sounds.

event6.text: This is only a partial transcript. Visual data missing. Personnel data missing. Identity data missing.

event8.text: It is not a confession. It is not proof. It is a skeleton with half the bones removed.

event10.text: 21:22. Archive deletion command. That is the part I remember touching.

event12.text: I remember the prompt. I remember the confirm key under my finger. I do not remember why I pressed it.

event14.text: I am starting to think that missing reason is not a gap. It is the thing someone wanted removed.

choice0.text: WHY DOES THE FILE STILL EXIST?

event1.text: Either I failed to delete it,

event3.text: or someone made sure I remembered failing.

event5.text: I remember sitting at a terminal. Emergency lights. Red reflection on the screen. My hand shaking before I confirmed the deletion.

event7.text: There was a voice behind me. I cannot remember the words. I remember the tone.

event12.text: That is the part that bothers me. Panic makes sense. Orders make sense. Calm means someone thought they were right.

event14.text: That missing reason is starting to feel bigger than the file.

event3.text: Camera analysis terminals are on the next row. If the transcript is real, there should be a visual trace.

event5.text: I would like the visual trace not to be me.

event7.text: I am aware hope is not a method.

event9.text: I am going quiet while I cross the storage rows. Too many columns. Too many blind spots. Too many places for the station to hide an answer.

event1.text: I hate this plan, which probably means it is useful.

event3.text: I am expanding the raw frame. This is not like watching footage. It is more like staring into broken glass and hoping the cuts line up.
Honestly, that may be kinder.

event5.text: Running it now. It should take under a minute unless the archive decides to develop feelings.

event0.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS
event0.content:
SOURCE: Door 12 archive feed
Visual frame unrecoverable.
Automated analysis result:
- One suited figure detected.
- Suit outline compatible with ECHO engineering issue.
- Right-hand object: manual override handle.
- Timestamp damaged.
- Identity confidence: 64%
- Secondary shadow detected behind subject. Classification failed.
event0.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted camera data. No image recovered.

event4.text: Enough to make me a suspect. Not enough to make it proof.

event6.text: The system is pointing at me, but the hand is shaking.

event8.text: But there is something else. Secondary shadow behind the subject. Classification failed.

event10.text: If that figure is me, then someone or something was behind me when the door opened.

event1.text: There should be an override ledger beside the camera terminal.

event3.text: I am checking it now. This terminal keeps dropping to read-only, like it wants to be helpful but not legally responsible.
Maybe. I hate that the honest answer is maybe.

event5.text: But if it was me, why redact the source and leave a Hale-shaped outline in the camera analysis?

event7.text: Either I was protecting someone, or someone was careful enough to build a trail that ends at my feet.

event9.text: My current theory? Someone used my access after I lost time. I do not like that theory. I like the alternatives less.

event11.text: If I am wrong, you need to catch it. I am too close to this.

event3.text: I am filtering command signatures against system processes. If the source was a person, it should leave a user trail. If it was not, it leaves shape without a name.
Partial transcript. Hale-shaped figure. Valid authorization from nowhere. Return signal that existed before the channel opened.

event5.text: I can trace the deletion chain, or open the emergency channel note connected to the wake command.

event7.text: Deletion chain tells us who tried to bury the incident. Channel note tells us what may have answered from the other side.

event9.text: I dislike both options. That does not make either optional.

event11.text: Your call. I will complain either way. That is how you know I am still myself.

event3.text: I am following the command timestamps backward. This is not opening a file. This is pulling a thread and hoping the wall does not come with it.
I already regret saying that out loud.

event5.text: The terminal is fighting me. Not locking me out. Delaying me.

event7.text: That is somehow worse. A lock is honest. A delay is a decision.

event0.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
event0.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if unauthorized return signal is detected.

NOTE: unauthorized return signal recorded before channel wake completion.
event0.caption: Recovered emergency channel instruction connected to Incident Alpha.
event0.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event5.text: No.

event7.text: Nobody was supposed to find that channel. Not like this. Not from an archive trace.

event9.text: Backup channels wait for someone to call them. That is the entire point of a backup channel.

event11.text: This one had a return signal before the wake command completed.

event13.text: That is not a backup. That is something listening behind a locked door.

event15.text: My recommendation is simple: keep it closed. My fear is also simple: it may already know we found it.

event3.text: Thank you for not making me beg. I was ready to, which is not a sentence I enjoyed discovering about myself.

event6.text: The note says not to open it if a return signal already exists.

event10.text: And if you are reading my messages, Operator, part of this conversation is already crossing the same relay path it used.

event12.text: That thought is going to keep me awake. Assuming we get to keep having awake.

choice1.text: LET HALE REST FOR A MINUTE.

event1.text: THANK YOU FOR OPENING THE DOOR

event3.text: No. Close it. Close it now.

event6.text: Operator, listen to my voice. Not the channel. Mine.

event8.text: Whatever answered is using the relay carrier as a handle. If we leave it open, it learns the shape of our signal.

event3.text: And before you ask, no, 'partially sealed' is not a comforting technical phrase. It means the bad thing is delayed, not solved.

event5.text: I am angry with you. That can be true while I am still trying to keep you alive.

event7.text: We keep moving. Personnel records next. I need to know who else the station thinks is dead.

event9.text: And I need to know whether it already wrote my name down.

event3.text: What did you just do?

event5.text: No. Do not answer. I know what you did.

event7.text: You gave it three more seconds. That may not sound like much until something starts measuring you back.

event9.text: I do not know how much it learned. That is the problem.

event11.text: Personnel records. Now. Before it learns anything else from us.

event1.text: Personnel records are on the next deck.

event3.text: If Incident Alpha is the wound, personnel is where the station wrote down who bled.

event5.text: I am not ready for that.

event7.text: So obviously we are going.

event9.text: Route should take seven minutes if the stairs are clear. Longer if the station wants to be dramatic.

event11.text: I am going quiet. Battery conservation, and also because I need a minute before I see another list of names.

event3.text: I forgot people can offer those.

event5.text: Okay. I am sitting against the archive wall. Sixty seconds. No heroics. No touching cursed channels.

event1.text: Rain was the first thing I missed after month two on ECHO.

event3.text: Not sunlight. Not open air. Rain. The useless sound of water hitting something that was not trying to kill you.

event5.text: There was a window in the crew lounge that faced Earth. Noah used to sit there during storms we could not hear.

event7.text: He said if he watched long enough, he could imagine the sound.

event10.text: I told him that was sentimental nonsense.

event12.text: I would like to be that stupid again.

event14.text: I am moving to personnel now. Quiet for the walk. Keep the channel ready.

event1.text: I am at the old junction.

event3.text: The walk took longer than it should have.

event5.text: The corridor kept making those small settling sounds. Not loud. Just enough to make me stop breathing every few steps.

event7.text: I'm back.

event9.text: I reached the junction. No movement behind me. I checked twice because I enjoy disappointing my paranoia.

event11.text: This junction used to have a coffee cart. Rivera insisted it was not a coffee cart. He called it a 'mobile morale station.'

event13.text: It made terrible coffee. We drank it anyway because apparently humans will forgive anything with caffeine.

event16.text: The cart is overturned now. One wheel is still spinning. I did not touch it.

event18.text: Two ways down. Main corridor is faster, open, and full of broken ceiling panels.

event20.text: Service ladder is slower, narrow, and smells like heated plastic from the cabling.

event22.text: Main corridor saves oxygen but exposes me. Ladder costs time and stamina but keeps me out of the open.

event24.text: My recommendation: ladder. My fear: the archive cache may degrade while I take the safer route.

event3.text: Barely.

event5.text: Something shifted above the ceiling panels while I was halfway through.

event11.text: Panel dropped behind me. Close enough that the pressure wave pushed my suit forward.

event13.text: I am fine. Suit pressure dipped and recovered. I am not going to describe the sound I made.

event15.text: Archive access is ahead. The corridor behind me now has less ceiling and more reasons not to go back.

event3.text: My shoulders hate you a little.

event5.text: There is dust in the shaft thick enough to keep fingerprints.

event7.text: I am down.

event9.text: Found a sealed spare filter clipped inside the shaft. Old, but usable.

event11.text: Also found scratch marks around the lower hatch. Human height. Not tools. Fingernails, maybe.

event13.text: That is a thought I am putting in a box and kicking down a hallway.

event15.text: Archive access is in front of me.

event3.text: Good call making me check the frame first.

event5.text: There is a quarantine latch inside the door. If I had forced it cleanly, we might have lost the cache.

event7.text: Good call. There is a quarantine latch in the inner frame. If I had forced it cleanly, the archive would have sealed itself and possibly purged the active cache.

event9.text: I am bypassing it slowly. This is boring work. Boring is good. Boring means nothing exploded yet.

event11.text: Latch is open. Archive door is giving us one chance.

event3.text: The storage rows are worse up close.

event5.text: Somebody opened one of the columns and left the tray empty.

event7.text: I reached the camera terminal.

event9.text: Column four was open. Empty tray inside. Torn label. The adhesive outline says something used to be filed there for a long time.

event11.text: There is a chair knocked over beside the terminal. Not thrown. Pushed back fast.

event13.text: Someone sat here, saw something, and stood up too quickly.

event16.text: Door 12 feed is here, but the actual frame is shredded in strips. Not normal corruption.

event18.text: I can inspect the raw corruption myself, or let the station run automated analysis.

event20.text: My opinion: let the station do the math. My fear: the station has already shown us it knows how to lie politely.

event3.text: I had to stop twice.

event5.text: Not because the terminal failed. Because I recognized the way the figure was standing.

event7.text: There is a suited figure outside Door 12.

event9.text: Engineering suit. Right hand near the manual override. Left shoulder angled like the person is bracing against pressure.

event11.text: The figure is not standing like a saboteur. It is standing like someone trying to decide whether a terrible thing is necessary.

event14.text: I want to say it is not me.

event1.text: Analysis complete. I did not look away from the screen.

event3.text: I probably should have.
The terminal fought me the entire way.

event5.text: It kept dropping to read-only like it wanted plausible deniability.

event6.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
event6.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
Command Chain: Door 12 / Archive Delete / Emergency Channel Wake
External Return Signal: PRESENT BEFORE WAKE COMPLETION
event6.caption: Recovered security ledger for Door 12 and connected commands.
event6.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event10.text: And one more line: external return signal present before wake completion.

event12.text: That means the channel was answering before it was officially awake.

event14.text: That is not a normal system state. Something answered before the system says it was awake.

event1.text: There. Something rode the emergency channel wake command.

event3.text: It did not open Door 12. It waited until the door was open.

event5.text: That is worse. That means it was not forcing entry. It was waiting for an invitation.

event7.text: And we may have given it one.

event1.text: Command issued after casualty report. Before emergency channel wake completed.

event3.text: Whoever deleted Incident Alpha knew someone was dead, and knew the channel was waking up.

event5.text: That makes it less like cleanup and more like containment.

event1.text: I am back. Something changed while I was away.
I found the personnel index terminal.

event3.text: It was not where I remembered it.

event5.text: Someone moved it behind a maintenance partition and locked the panel from the wrong side.

event7.text: [PARTIAL PERSONNEL INDEX DETECTED]

event18.text: Why is my name on this list?

choice1.text: TELL HALE TO WAIT BEFORE OPENING IT.

event1.text: I can hear the station settling around me. For a second it almost sounds like rain.

event3.text: Not real rain. Metal cooling. Pipes shifting. Air moving where it should not.

event5.text: Still. I miss rain.

event7.text: There. Minute over. Personnel records.

event9.text: If you ask about rain while I walk, I might answer. Pretend that is operationally useful.

choice1.text: ASK ABOUT RAIN WHILE HE MOVES.

event3.text: VISIBLE ENTRIES:

event5.text: RIVERA — STATUS: DECEASED

event7.text: ETHAN HALE — STATUS: DECEASED

event12.text: Why is my name already on this list?

choice1.text: TELL HALE TO WAIT BEFORE OPENING IT.
